sources: Avoma call 2025-12-18 (Zycus - John Deere Discussion), speaker Karen Powers
metrics.narrative: Zycus proposed quantified ROI (e.g. 40% sourcing cycle-time reduction, 60% enhanced bids) in the 18 Dec 2025 pricing walkthrough, but Karen Powers directly challenged the figures as 'pretty optimistic' and asked to 'reset expectations based on current environment,' requesting benchmarks and a comparable-customer reference call before accepting them (18 Dec 2025 call). Zycus promised a business-case value-engineering sheet, sample output, and a reference call by 5 Jan 2026, but no read call or note confirms these were delivered or that the ROI was subsequently validated.

champion.narrative: Karen Pagorek (Global Strategic Sourcing Manager) has carried the day-to-day relationship through every 2026 touchpoint (28 Jan, 24 Feb, 31 Mar, 11 May) and is named in the Next Step as the verified path to agreement alongside Eduardo Viera. However, she is also the one who delivered the negative update that the POC would not move forward (11 May 2026), and there is no evidence she has brokered access to the CFO or secured budget backing -- champion strength reads as developing/at-risk rather than confirmed.

competition.narrative: Salesforce lists Oracle, SAP Ariba, and 'Others' as competitors, though no call evidence in the read set corroborates active bake-offs against either named ERP vendor. The concretely evidenced rivals are: the existing in-house build (TCS homegrown quoting plus OpenAI/Ultrametrics partnerships, confirmed pain source since 24 Jun 2025); GEP, which the rep's own Next Step names as the target to counter via a Spend Analysis pilot; and, most urgently, an unidentified internal/external 'I/O provider' that Karen Pagorek disclosed on 11 May 2026 is under active deep-dive evaluation and was the stated reason the POC was paused.

identify_pain.narrative: Karen Powers clearly articulated the core pain on 24 Jun 2025: John Deere's homegrown quoting tool (TCS) requires extensive customization ('deorizing') to fit purchased solutions, calling this 'a nightmare,' and described a felt need to blend homegrown tools with purchased/partnered AI capabilities (OpenAI, Ultrametrics, WorldLink) to accelerate transformation without regressing on efficiency.

sources: Avoma call 2025-12-18 ('this is not anywhere near a final commercial proposal... very much budgetary estimate')
paper_process.narrative: No MSA, Order Form, SOW, or any commercial-paper discussion appears anywhere in the record. The deal remains in Shortlisted/POC evaluation with only a budgetary (non-final) pricing proposal shared on 18 Dec 2025.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Executive Sponsor: Eduardo Viera, Joshua Jepsen)
economic_buyer.narrative: Two Executive Sponsors are named on the Salesforce contact roles: Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Global Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services) and Joshua Jepsen (SVP & CFO). Eduardo held one direct 1:1 with the deal owner on 30 Sep 2025 discussing make-vs-buy strategy and a December workshop, and appeared on the attendee list of the Nov 2025 workshops, but has had no confirmed touchpoint since 17 Nov 2025 (~234 days / ~7.7 months as of today). Joshua Jepsen has never appeared in any read call, note, or attendee list across the entire history. The Next Step explicitly names Eduardo as needing 'a jolt to action,' confirming the rep's own read that access to power is stalled.

decision_process.narrative: Eduardo Viera stated on 30 Sep 2025 that John Deere was 'not looking at a competitive tender' and preferred a collaborative/partnership path over an RFP, given active in-house development. However, as of 11 May 2026 Karen Pagorek disclosed a parallel deep dive on an unidentified internal/external I/O provider, and the POC was paused for reprioritization -- indicating the internal decision process is neither closed nor clearly mapped, and no budget sign-off pathway has been documented.

sources: Next_Step__c (three-prong approach, target scorecard already drafted)
decision_criteria.narrative: A Nov 2025 workshop series (3 Nov and 17 Nov 2025, both logged but not recorded) was intended to fit-check Zycus modules against John Deere's roadmap. On the 18 Dec 2025 call, Karen Powers asked Zycus to clearly differentiate iSource/e-auctions from agent-driven autonomous sourcing, which Zycus committed to clarifying. A three-prong pilot scorecard (Spend Analysis vs GEP, iSource POC, ANA pilot for MRO) was drafted per the rep's Next Step, but no confirmed, buyer-signed set of evaluation criteria exists in the record.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 229 (2027-02-22). Deal created 2025-06-06, age 398 days. In Shortlisted stage since unknown date (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c not retrieved). Close date has slipped at least four times per Next_Step__c. Current stage (Shortlisted) requires moving to Vendor Selected within ~60 days to stay on pace for Feb 2027 close, but buyer paused POC in May 2026 to evaluate internal alternative and has not resumed. Last verified activity 2026-07-20, 12 days in the future from today (2026-07-08), indicating scheduled re-engagement call. No two-way buyer engagement since May 2026 call where Karen stated POC 'will not move forward' and they are 're-assessing priorities'. Forward slip risk high: deal has been open 13 months, remains in early-mid stage, and Eduardo (EB) has been unresponsive since ~September 2025.
evidence: Best Case forecast is indefensible: no Economic Buyer engagement in 9 months (Eduardo Viera last contact ~September 2025 per Next_Step), no CFO engagement ever (Joshua Jepsen listed Executive Sponsor but never participated per call history), no commercial or budget approval pathway confirmed, and POC paused May 2026 with buyer exploring internal build or competitive alternative (Next_Step: 'ramifications: on hold, in-house build, or competitive loss'). Champion Karen Pagorek remains engaged but has no budget authority and stated POC 'will not move forward' on 2026-05-11. Deal should be Pipeline until EB re-engages and budget path is locked.

Single-threaded through Karen Pagorek (Global Strategic Sourcing Manager, Influencer role) since May 2026. Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Economic Buyer) and Joshua Jepsen (SVP & CFO, Executive Sponsor) are not engaged. Gia Duke (Director WW Indirect Materials, Evaluator) and Zachary Wenzel (Director Supply Chain, Evaluator) last appeared on calls in December 2025.

POC for ANA and Spend Analytics was delivered March 31, 2026, feedback was 'positive' per Next_Step, but on May 11, 2026 Karen stated they are 're-assessing priorities' and POC 'will not move forward'. Buyer is 'doing a deep dive on another eternal I/O provider' (unidentified, possibly internal build per Next_Step). Arthur reached out to schedule call to understand ramifications; call accepted for July 20, 2026 per Next_Step.

Buyer challenged Zycus's quantified ROI (40% sourcing cycle-time reduction) as 'pretty optimistic' on 2025-12-18 call and requested benchmarks and proof. Arthur committed to deliver benchmarks and reference call by early January 2026. POC delivered March 31 but no confirmation that ROI validation occurred before May pause.
north_star_verdict.headline: POC paused since May 2026 while buyer explores internal I/O alternative; Eduardo Viera (Economic Buyer) silent for 9 months, no budget pathway confirmed, and close date now 229 days out despite multiple slips. Rep-initiated re-engagement scheduled for July 20th has yet to occur; outcome uncertain.

flags: Convert the 2026-07-20 call into a limited-scope proof-of-value pilot (30-day iSource RFX or ANA MRO negotiation) with a specific business outcome (e.g. 3-5% savings on MRO tail spend, or 2-week cycle-time reduction on a strategic sourcing event) — bypass the stalled business-case and benchmarking discussion by proving ROI in 30 days, faster than the internal I/O provider can deliver.

Identify and neutralize the 'eternal I/O provider' threat — Karen stated on 2026-05-11 they are deep-diving another provider (unnamed); Zycus must discover who it is (internal build? partner? competitor?) and position against it with speed-to-value and modular integration wedge on the 2026-07-20 call or the deal will be lost to that alternative.

Economic buyer Eduardo Viera has not engaged since approximately September 2025 (inferred from Next_Step 'need a Jolt to action'); no direct relationship, no budget approval pathway mapped (source: SF Next_Step__c multiple dates, Avoma call history 2025-06 through 2025-12 shows no Eduardo attendance).

CFO Joshua Jepsen, the named Executive Sponsor contact role, has never appeared on a single call in the deal's 13-month history; if the internal evaluation reaches budget sign-off, Zycus has no established relationship at that gate.

Single-thread risk: Karen Pagorek is sole active contact as of May-July 2026; all other stakeholders (Gia Duke, Zachary Wenzel, Karen Powers) last engaged December 2025 or earlier (source: Avoma call history, SF Next_Step__c).

Benchmarks and reference call promised January 2026 to validate 40% cycle-time reduction ROI claim remain undelivered as of POC pause May 2026, leaving value case unproven (source: Avoma call 2025-12-18, SF Next_Step__c).

Champion Karen Pagorek is currently cooling while she evaluates an unnamed internal I/O provider; win the deal by arming her with a head-to-head comparison before her 20 Jul reconnect, not by waiting passively for the outcome.

Executive Sponsor Eduardo Viera has engaged only twice in ten months (30 Sep 2025, 12 Jun 2026); a direct executive-to-executive reinforcement now, while he says 'Zycus is one of those' options, is the highest-leverage lever before John Deere's internal decision lands.
